title: achillea setacea f. dolopica
intro: Achillea setacea f. dolopica, also known as White Yarrow, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family, native to Europe, Asia, and parts of North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of White Yarrow include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for stress and anxiety relief. In herbal preparations, White Yarrow is often used as a tea or infused oil, and can also be found in tinctures and salves, where its medicinal properties can be effectively utilized.
